title: Leuzea centauroides
taxonomy: Leuzea centauroides is classified as a flowering plant, specifically belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant is also known as Centaurea leuzeoides and is native to mountainous regions of Europe. The species is further classified as a member of the subfamily Carduoideae, which includes thistle-like plants. Within the family Asteraceae, Leuzea centauroides is a part of the tribe Cardueae, a group of plants that are typically prickly and produce small flowers. The exact classification can vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted that Leuzea centauroides is a member of the family Asteraceae.

It is believed to have adaptogenic properties, helping the body to adapt to stress and promote overall well-being. side_effects: Leuzea centauroides is known to cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea when consumed in large amounts. intro: Leuzea centauroides, also known as Siberian ginseng or Krestin, is a plant species in the genus Leuzea, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its adaptogenic properties, providing health benefits such as stress relief, improved physical performance, and enhanced immune function. The bioactive constituents of Leuzea centauroides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
